data.name: NoGlobalVariablesTitle
data.value: Avoid global variables in AL objects

data.name: NoGlobalVariablesMessageFormat
data.value: Global variable '{0}' should be avoided. Pass variables as parameters or use local variables instead.

data.name: NoGlobalVariablesDescription
data.value: Global variables in AL objects create implicit coupling between procedures and make code harder to test and maintain. Prefer passing values as parameters or using local variables.

data.name: CaptionTooltipOnPageTitle
data.value: Caption and ToolTip should be defined on the table field, not on the page field

data.name: CaptionTooltipOnPageMessageFormat
data.value: '{0}' should not be set on the page field. Define it on the table field instead so it is shared across all pages.

data.name: CaptionTooltipOnPageDescription
data.value: Caption and ToolTip properties defined on page fields are not reused when the same field appears on other pages. Define these properties on the table field instead so that all pages inherit them automatically, reducing duplication and keeping translations in one place.

data.name: CaptionTooltipOnPageRemoveCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Remove '{0}' from page field

data.name: CaptionTooltipOnPageMoveCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Move '{0}' to table field

data.name: CaptionTooltipOnPageMoveExtCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Move '{0}' to table extension

data.name: OpenBraceOnSameLineTitle
data.value: Opening brace must be on the same line as the declaration

data.name: OpenBraceOnSameLineMessageFormat
data.value: Opening brace '{{' must be on the same line as the declaration, not on a new line.

data.name: OpenBraceOnSameLineDescription
data.value: The opening brace of a field, column, or dataitem declaration must appear on the same line as the closing parenthesis. Placing the brace on a new line wastes vertical space and reduces readability.

data.name: OpenBraceOnSameLineCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Move opening brace to same line

data.name: UseRestClientTitle
data.value: Use the "Rest Client" codeunit instead of raw HTTP types

data.name: UseRestClientMessageFormat
data.value: Variable of type '{0}' detected. Use the "Rest Client" codeunit instead of raw HTTP types for simpler, more maintainable HTTP communication.

data.name: UseRestClientDescription
data.value: The "Rest Client" codeunit provides a higher-level abstraction over raw HTTP types (HttpClient, HttpRequestMessage, HttpResponseMessage, HttpContent, HttpHeaders). It simplifies HTTP communication by handling content creation, request execution, and response parsing in fewer lines of code with less boilerplate.

data.name: InitializeRestClientWithHandlerTitle
data.value: Rest Client must be initialized with a handler codeunit from the current app

data.name: InitializeRestClientWithHandlerMessageFormat
data.value: RestClient.Initialize() must pass a handler codeunit defined in the current app. Create a codeunit that implements the "Http Client Handler" interface to centralize HTTP client configuration.

data.name: InitializeRestClientWithHandlerDescription
data.value: The "Rest Client" codeunit should be initialized with a custom handler codeunit defined in the current app. This ensures HTTP client configuration (timeouts, default headers, retry policies) is centralized and consistent across all HTTP calls. Without a local handler, the default system handler is used, which may not match the app's requirements.

data.name: NoExitWithDefaultValueTitle
data.value: Do not use exit() with an explicit default value

data.name: NoExitWithDefaultValueMessageFormat
data.value: exit() is called with the default value '{0}'. Use a bare exit; instead — AL returns the type's default automatically.

data.name: NoExitWithDefaultValueDescription
data.value: When a procedure's exit statement passes the type's default value (false for Boolean, 0 for Integer, '' for Text/Code), the explicit value is redundant. A bare exit; (without an argument) already returns the default. Removing the redundant value improves readability.
